testcase.name: test_main_download_video_with_quality

failure.message: assert 1 == 0 + where 1 = <Result AttributeError("'Downloader' object has no attribute 'http'")>.exit_code

testcase.failure:
def test_main_download_video_with_quality(): runner = CliRunner() with patch( "apyrat.apyrat.Downloader._get_available_qualities", return_value=["480", "720", "1080"], ) as mock_qualities: result = runner.invoke( main, [ "https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I", "-q", "720", ], ) > assert result.exit_code == 0 E assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result AttributeError("'Downloader' object has no attribute 'http'")>.exit_code
tests/cli_test.py:21: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_main_download_video_without_quality

failure.message: assert 1 == 0 + where 1 = <Result AttributeError("'Downloader' object has no attribute 'http'")>.exit_code

testcase.failure:
def test_main_download_video_without_quality(): runner = CliRunner() with patch( "apyrat.apyrat.Downloader._get_available_qualities", return_value=["480", "720", "1080"], ), patch("click.prompt", return_value="720"): result = runner.invoke(main, ["https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I"]) > assert result.exit_code == 0 E assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result AttributeError("'Downloader' object has no attribute 'http'")>.exit_code
tests/cli_test.py:32: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_get_quality_available
testcase.failure:
def test_get_quality_available(): > downloader = Downloader("https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/cli_test.py:36: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ apyrat/apyrat.py:40: in __init__ self.videos = self._get_available_videos() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ apyrat/apyrat.py:63: in _get_available_videos return [self._get_single_video_qualities(self.video_uid)] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <apyrat.apyrat.Downloader object at 0x106984bd0>, video_uid = 'qur3I' playlist_id = None
def _get_single_video_qualities(self, video_uid, playlist_id=None): query = f"?playlist={playlist_id}&pr=1&mf=1" if playlist_id else "" > response = self.http.request( ^^^^^^^^^ 'GET', f"{API_BASE_URL}/video/video/show/videohash/{video_uid}{query}", headers=prepare_headers(), ) E AttributeError: 'Downloader' object has no attribute 'http'
apyrat/apyrat.py:96: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_get_available_videos_single_video
testcase.failure:
mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4394663120'>
@patch("requests.get") def test_get_available_videos_single_video(mock_get): mock_get.return_value.json.return_value = { "data": { "attributes": { "title": "My Video Title", "file_link_all": [ {"profile": "720p", "urls": ["https://link-to-video"]}, {"profile": "1080p", "urls": ["https://link-to-video"]}, ], } } } > downloader = Downloader("https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I", "outputfile") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

self = <apyrat.apyrat.Downloader object at 0x106716750>, video_uid = 'qur3I' playlist_id = None

apyrat/apyrat.py:96: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_download_when_file_exists
testcase.failure:
mock_wget = <MagicMock name='download' id='4407659600'> mock_isfile = <MagicMock name='isfile' id='4407658576'>
@patch("os.path.isfile", return_value=True) @patch("wget.download") def test_download_when_file_exists(mock_wget, mock_isfile): > downloader = Downloader("https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I", "outputfile") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
